experiences.intentions: The board is empty except for the active T near the top in its spawn orientation. With an empty field, the priority is efficient placement toward a flat stack and keeping future pieces flexible. Immediate threats are nonexistent. Candidate actions: hard_drop would place the T safely in the center quickly on a flat empty board, which is a strong neutral opening and preserves flexibility for upcoming J, Z, O, S pieces. Left/right would only reposition before eventual drop, and no specific column advantage is compelling yet. Rotations are unnecessary on an empty board. Soft_drop advances less efficiently than hard_drop. Therefore the best action now is to hard drop the T in its current central position.
experiences.tasks: Clear as many lines as possible in Tetris by placing tetrominoes strategically.

experiences.intentions: The board is nearly empty except for a T piece resting at the bottom center, creating a small 2-cell-high mound around columns 4-6. The active J is at the top in its spawn orientation. Goal is to place pieces cleanly and keep the stack flat for upcoming Z,O,S,I. A strong sub-goal is to move the J toward a side and later drop it vertically or tuck it to avoid building over the center. Among immediate actions, hard_drop would commit too early in the center, and rotations now are unnecessary before horizontal positioning. Moving left starts shifting the J away from the central T stack and preserves flexibility for future placements.
experiences.tasks: Clear as many lines as possible in Tetris by placing tetrominoes strategically.

experiences.intentions: The board is nearly empty except for a T piece resting at the bottom center, and the active J is high near the top in its horizontal orientation. Goal is to place the J efficiently to start building a flat, compact stack without creating holes. Since there is no immediate threat and horizontal J can fit cleanly onto the left side of the bottom T structure, the strongest move now is to commit the piece downward immediately. Hard dropping maximizes progress and is safe on this open board; lateral or rotation adjustments can wait only if needed, but current placement is already reasonable and there are no obstacles above.
experiences.tasks: Clear as many lines as possible in Tetris by placing tetrominoes strategically.

experiences.intentions: The active piece is a Z near the top in its horizontal spawn orientation, with a low uneven stack: J creates support around columns 3-5 and T occupies columns 4-6 at the bottom, leaving broad space elsewhere. Immediate threat is low since the board is mostly empty, so the goal is efficient placement to keep the surface flat and set up the upcoming O/S/I/L pieces. Candidate actions: hard_drop would commit the Z immediately, likely onto the right side creating a jagged profile; soft_drop also commits downward without improving alignment; rotations are not necessary yet because the current horizontal Z can be shifted into a cleaner slot. Moving right steers the Z toward the natural notch above the T/J structure and the open right half, preserving flexibility for O and I next. Therefore the best single action now is to move right.

Surface has a low center-left structure: J/O stack around columns 3-6, T and Z on the right, with open flat space across the left and upper board. experiences.intentions: Active T is high near center-top with empty space below, so no immediate collision threat. The stack is uneven in the lower half, with a vertical I at column 5 and a small cavity structure around the center-right. Goal is to place the T efficiently to improve future surface and potentially set up line clears without creating overhangs. Candidate moves: hard_drop now in current orientation would likely land centrally but may not be optimal without horizontal adjustment; rotating could prepare a notch fit later but there is no urgency while piece is still high. Moving right aligns the T over the center-right area near the I/OO structures, giving a better chance to slot it into the developing surface on subsequent turns. Left seems less promising given the L/S/J clutter on the left. So right is the best setup move now.

experiences.intentions: The active T is high and unobstructed near the upper-right. The stack below is low-mid with a central/right column built up; there is no immediate threat, so the goal is to place the T cleanly to preserve a flat surface and avoid creating holes. From the current spawn, moving right would hug the wall and likely make an awkward notch, while rotating now is unnecessary because the current flat-side-down T can still be shifted into useful positions later. Dropping immediately from here places it onto the existing right-side height in a straightforward, safe way and advances the game without risking misalignment.

experiences.intentions: Active piece is a horizontal I at the very top spanning columns 3-6, with a lot of empty space below and the existing stack concentrated lower down. The board surface has a notable vertical well at column 5 that already contains an I standing from rows 11-14, so immediately hard dropping the current I horizontally would just land flat on the empty upper field and be inefficient. Goal is to place this I to improve future line clears and avoid creating overhangs; the strongest sub-goal is to rotate it vertical so it can be guided into a useful column, likely the central well/nearby gap, on subsequent turns. Candidate actions: left/right only shift the current horizontal bar and do not exploit the open vertical space yet; soft_drop/hard_drop commit too early in a poor horizontal placement; no_op wastes tempo. Rotating right converts the I into a vertical piece in open space, preserving flexibility for later horizontal movement and a cleaner eventual drop.
experiences.tasks: Clear as many lines as possible in Tetris by placing tetrominoes strategically.

experiences.intentions: Active piece is a vertical I at column 5 near the top. The stack below has a clean 4-cell-wide vertical gap in the same column from rows 11-14, which this I can fill perfectly for strong placement and likely line progress. Immediate goal is to align and lock the I into that well safely. Candidate actions: left/right would misalign from the existing well, rotations would turn it horizontal and lose the fit, no_op delays without benefit. Dropping advances directly toward the target while preserving alignment; hard_drop secures the best placement now.

experiences.intentions: Active L is high at the top in rotation 0 around columns 4-6. The stack below has a tall vertical I in column 5 and uneven left-side structure, with no immediate danger because the piece is near spawn and the board top is open. Goal is to improve future placement while keeping flexibility for upcoming J,S,Z,J. Moving left positions the L toward the left structure where it can potentially tuck or support the uneven surface; moving right would conflict more with the central I column and be less useful. Rotating now is possible but not yet necessary; dropping now would commit to a poor high placement. Best immediate setup action is to shift left.

experiences.intentions: Active piece is an L near the top with plenty of fall distance. The stack below has a tall I-column around x=5 and uneven left-side structure; there is no immediate danger at the top. Goal is to place the L to improve the left-side surface and avoid creating overhangs. From the spawn orientation, moving left starts aligning the piece with the left shelf around rows 13-15, while dropping immediately would land too centrally and less usefully. Rotating now is possible later, but first shifting left preserves options and moves toward the best landing zone.
experiences.tasks: Clear as many lines as possible in Tetris by placing tetrominoes strategically.

experiences.intentions: Active piece is L near the upper-left, with plenty of air below and no immediate danger. The stack on the left already has an L-shaped cavity around columns 0-2 in rows 13-14, and orienting the current L vertically can help feed that side cleanly. Candidate actions: left shifts toward the useful left wall setup; right moves away from the existing left structure; rotations are possible but there is still height to move first before committing; dropping now would strand the piece high and create clutter. Best sub-goal is to guide the piece left toward the left-side placement opportunity.

experiences.intentions: Active piece is L near the upper left. The stack is low and there are no immediate threats, so the goal is to place this piece cleanly without creating overhangs while preparing for upcoming J/S/Z pieces. Candidate actions: hard_drop would commit immediately in the current horizontal orientation at the far left, creating a simple flat-ish placement on the left side with minimal risk; moving/rotating could refine placement, but there is no urgent need and current placement is already safe. Soft_drop/no_op are less efficient. Best immediate action is to lock in the safe left placement.

experiences.intentions: Active J is near the top in rotation 0 at columns around 4-6. The stack is low but uneven, with a tall I column around x=5 and useful cavities on the left/middle. Goal is to place this J to improve surface and avoid creating overhangs before upcoming S,Z,J,L. Immediate hard drop from current position would likely land awkwardly on the central column; moving left gives more flexibility to tuck J onto the left structures or flatten the mid-left on subsequent turns. Rotating now doesn’t clearly improve more than shifting toward the better landing zone, and soft/hard drop commits too early.

experiences.intentions: Active J is high near the top at columns 3-5. Surface below has a deep central well around column 3-4 and a tall I column at 5, so immediate threat is low because there is lots of vertical space. Goal is to place J to improve the left-center topology without creating overhangs; since we only choose one step now, the best sub-goal is to start shifting the piece toward the useful left-side cavity. Candidate actions: hard_drop would commit from a poor spawn position; soft_drop reduces maneuver time; rotations are not yet necessary because current flat-bottom J can still be moved later. Moving left begins aligning with the left-center gap and preserves future rotation options.
experiences.tasks: Clear as many lines as possible in Tetris by placing tetrominoes strategically.

experiences.intentions: Active J is high on the left with lots of air below; no immediate top-out threat. The stack below is uneven, with a central I column around x=5 and some left-side structure. Goal now is to place the J efficiently toward the left/center without wasting time; since the piece is still very high, dropping it downward is the most direct progress and preserves lateral/rotation options for later. Left/right/rotate now are premature, while hard drop would commit too early without full alignment.
experiences.tasks: Clear as many lines as possible in Tetris by placing tetrominoes strategically.

experiences.intentions: The active J piece is high on the left, currently horizontal with a tail on the left. The stack below is low in the upper field, so there is no immediate danger. There is open space to the left wall, and future pieces S,Z,J,L benefit from preserving a flat surface and avoiding awkward overhangs near the center I-column. A good sub-goal is to move this J toward the left side before committing downward, where it can later slot cleanly without worsening the central well. Among actions, left advances that placement plan immediately; rotate actions are not yet necessary, and dropping now would lock it in a less considered central-left position.
experiences.tasks: Clear as many lines as possible in Tetris by placing tetrominoes strategically.

experiences.intentions: Active piece is a J near the upper-left, with existing stacks concentrated lower and a vertical I column around x=5. The immediate goal is to place the J cleanly without creating holes; the best opportunity is to move it down toward the uneven left-center surface before locking. Horizontal movement now is more useful than rotating in place, since current orientation already fits and there is open space below. A soft drop advances toward placement while preserving flexibility next turn, unlike hard drop which may commit too early.

experiences.intentions: The stack is uneven with a tall central-right column already occupied by I blocks, and the active J is near the upper left of that column. There is no immediate line clear available by dropping now, and hard-dropping in the current orientation would likely create overhangs on the left-center. My goal is to place the J to smooth the left-center surface and avoid increasing the existing central spike. Rotating right to orientation 1 makes the J a vertical shape with a left protrusion that can better fit the cavity around columns 2-4 than the spawn orientation. Compared with left/right/soft drop, rotating now improves future placement options before committing downward.
experiences.tasks: Clear as many lines as possible in Tetris by placing tetrominoes strategically.

experiences.intentions: Active piece is a J in vertical orientation around column 2-4, high above the stack. Surface below has a central vertical I-column at x=5 and uneven left structures; no immediate line clear is available this turn. Goal is to place the J to support the upcoming S,Z,J,L sequence while avoiding creating holes. Rotating right to horizontal-top J (rotation 2) is the best setup: it can then be shifted and dropped into a flatter notch more flexibly than the current vertical shape. Left/right now are less committal but don't improve fit as much as getting the better orientation first; dropping now would likely strand the J awkwardly on the left bump.

experiences.intentions: Active piece is a vertical I in a mostly open upper field, currently in a 1-wide column around x=5. The surface below is jagged with some wells; preserving flexibility and avoiding an early hard lock in a mediocre spot is best. Rotating horizontally here would place the I across row 9 and is valid, creating future placement options and likely preparing to bridge or clear lines better than staying vertical. Candidate moves: left/right just shift without improving shape yet; soft/hard drop would commit prematurely; no_op wastes a turn. Best sub-goal is to orient the I for stronger placement before moving/dropping.

experiences.intentions: Active piece is an S near the top, currently horizontal at columns 4-5/5-6. The stack below is uneven with a tall central I-column around x=5 and relatively open space on both sides. Immediate threat is low because the piece is high and board top is empty. Goal is to place the S efficiently without creating extra holes; near-term sub-goal is to move it toward the left-center where there is an existing S/J structure and more support, instead of dropping prematurely onto the central spike. Candidate actions: hard_drop/soft_drop would likely land awkwardly on the central column and create bumpiness; rotation to vertical could be useful later but not necessary before shifting. Moving left starts aligning the S with the left-side contour and preserves flexibility for later rotation/drop. Right heads toward less supportive open area. Best action now: left.
experiences.tasks: Clear as many lines as possible in Tetris by placing tetrominoes strategically.

experiences.intentions: Active piece is S near the top-left/center, currently horizontal at rows 2-3. Surface below has a notable vertical well around columns 3-4 and clutter on the right; immediate danger is low since stack is far from the top. Goal is to place the S efficiently to smooth the left-center surface and avoid creating overhangs. Rotating to vertical would let it fit more cleanly into the left-center notch if moved later, but this turn only one action is allowed. Compared with left/right/soft_drop, rotating now preserves flexibility and sets up a stronger placement over the uneven left-center terrain; hard_drop now would commit too early from a high position. So rotate_right is the best preparatory move.
experiences.tasks: Clear as many lines as possible in Tetris by placing tetrominoes strategically.

experiences.intentions: Active piece is S in vertical orientation near the upper-middle, with open air below and no immediate danger at the top. The surface has a notable notch around columns 3-5 above the left-middle stack; placing S flat would better match that contour than keeping it vertical. Goal is to set up a clean stack and avoid awkward overhangs before committing to a drop. Candidate actions: left/right would only shift the current vertical S; soft/hard drop would lock a less useful orientation now; no_op wastes tempo. Rotating left or right both move to the flat S orientation shown, which is better for future placement. Choose rotate_right.
experiences.tasks: Clear as many lines as possible in Tetris by placing tetrominoes strategically.

experiences.intentions: Active piece is an S near the upper-left/center, currently around columns 2-5. The stack below has a vertical I-column around col 5 and uneven left-side terrain; there is no immediate top-out threat, so the priority is improving placement before committing. Candidate moves: hard_drop now would likely strand the S high on rough terrain; soft_drop also commits downward without improving alignment. Moving left could prepare a tuck over the left structures, but the current horizontal S often places better after becoming vertical to fit narrow notches. Rotating right changes the S into a vertical orientation that can better navigate around the central I-column and gives more future flexibility before dropping. Sub-goal now is setup, not commitment.
experiences.tasks: Clear as many lines as possible in Tetris by placing tetrominoes strategically.

experiences.intentions: Current piece is S near the upper-middle, interacting with a vertical I column around x=5. Surface below is uneven with a useful notch to the left-center. Goal is to place the S cleanly without creating overhangs and ideally fit it into the left-center contour before the stack gets higher. Candidate actions: left shifts the S toward the available left-center pocket; right moves it into/against the I column and is less useful; rotations do not immediately improve fit as the current horizontal S already aligns reasonably with the contour. Dropping now would commit too early. Best immediate sub-goal is to translate left first.

experiences.intentions: Active piece is a vertical I at column 5, with only one valid rotation state here, so rotation is unavailable. The immediate opportunity is the open vertical well in column 4 (0-indexed x=4) running down to row 10, where a vertical I would fit cleanly and complete the nearly full line around row 10 while also tucking the piece into a natural gap. Moving left now shifts the I toward that well; right has no obvious benefit, dropping now would strand it in the current column, and rotation collides. Sub-goal: align the I with the central well before dropping.

experiences.intentions: Active piece is Z near the top with current horizontal placement spanning columns 4-7; rotating right would create a vertical Z around columns 5-6. The stack below is uneven with a central open well around column 5 and no immediate danger at the top. Goal is to place the current Z to improve surface fit for upcoming J/L/S/O pieces while avoiding sealing awkward gaps. Candidate actions: hard_drop now would likely leave the Z high on the left-mid surface and may not fit optimally; soft_drop commits downward before alignment; rotate_left/right changes shape but not yet position enough; right moves toward the central/right contour where a Z can likely slot better above the T/ZZ area; left moves away from the best landing zone. Best immediate sub-goal is shift right before dropping.

experiences.intentions: Current active piece is Z near the top-right, already in a valid vertical orientation hugging the existing right-side contour. The board has uneven stacks but no immediate danger at the spawn area, so the priority is to place the Z efficiently without creating extra overhangs. Candidate moves: rotating left/right would switch to the flatter horizontal form, which appears less aligned with the right-side cavity; moving left/right could improve placement later but risks missing the clean vertical tuck already available. Soft drop advances toward locking while preserving future adjustments, whereas hard drop may commit too early without checking lateral fit. Best sub-goal is to continue descending in the current useful orientation and keep flexibility.

experiences.intentions: Current piece is Z near the upper-right, already vertical (matching rotation 1). The stack has a tall central I-column and uneven surface, but there is open space immediately to the right of the active Z. Goal is to place this Z cleanly without creating extra overhangs; with next pieces J,L,S,O, preserving flexibility on the right side is useful. Candidate actions: left would move into/over the central column area and likely worsen fit; rotations switch to horizontal and seem less immediately useful above this contour; dropping now would commit before aligning. Moving right shifts the vertical Z toward the open right wall, likely setting up a cleaner drop into the right-side notch on subsequent turns.

experiences.intentions: Active J is high near columns 4-6. Surface below has a clear vertical well around column 4 in the upper half and more clutter lower down; there is no immediate danger at the top, so the goal is to place this piece cleanly without creating overhangs and ideally set up the upcoming L/S/O/Z. Dropping now in the current orientation would likely strand the J on uneven support near the center-right. Rotating right to orientation 1 makes the J a vertical hook, which is better suited to slide/drop into a notch or alongside the central stack on subsequent moves. Left/right now is less committal but rotation improves future fit most directly.
experiences.tasks: Clear as many lines as possible in Tetris by placing tetrominoes strategically.

experiences.intentions: Active J is vertical at columns 5-6 near the top with ample space below. Surface has a notable vertical well around column 4 above the lower stack; moving the J left can help position it to tuck and reduce unevenness, while immediate hard/soft drop in current column would stack on the central I column and worsen the peak. Rotating now is less urgent than shifting toward a better landing zone. Best sub-goal is to improve placement options before committing. Therefore move left.
experiences.tasks: Clear as many lines as possible in Tetris by placing tetrominoes strategically.

experiences.intentions: Active J is near the top, currently vertical with a 2x2-style top in rotation 1. The stack is uneven but not near top-out; biggest immediate opportunity is to place the J cleanly without creating overhangs, ideally toward the left-center where there is open air above existing structure. From the spawn position, moving/rotating before locking matters more than dropping immediately. Rotating right would make the J horizontal with foot on the left, which fits more flexibly onto the left-center contours than the current vertical shape; left/right alone with current orientation is less committal, and hard/soft drop now would likely create a tall pillar. Best sub-goal is to orient the piece for a cleaner placement first.
experiences.tasks: Clear as many lines as possible in Tetris by placing tetrominoes strategically.

experiences.intentions: Active piece is an L near the top, currently around columns 4-6. The stack below is uneven but the upper field is mostly open, so immediate survival risk is low. Goal now is to place the L to avoid creating holes and preferably prepare for upcoming S/O/Z/T pieces. Among immediate actions, rotating right to orientation 1 makes the L vertical with its foot to the lower right, which is a flexible shape for sliding into the central-right area on subsequent moves and avoids committing to a premature horizontal placement. Left/right alone do less to improve fit yet, and hard/soft drop now would likely place it poorly high over open space. So the best current move is to rotate first.
